coin_profile.name: Ethereum
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: Ethereum is the leading smart contract platform and the foundation of decentralised finance (DeFi). It transitioned to proof-of-stake in 2022 (The Merge), making it energy-efficient. ETH is used to pay for computation on the network and is deflationary post-EIP-1559, with a portion of fees burned with every transaction. It underpins the majority of DeFi, NFT, and Layer 2 ecosystems.

moat.rationale: Ethereum's moat is exceptionally strong due to 15+ years of accumulated security, unmatched developer ecosystem lock-in, and settlement-layer dominance across DeFi (90%+ of TVL depends on ETH rails). While technically forkable, the network effects are insurmountable—switching costs for developers and capital are prohibitive, economic finality is proven, and regulatory clarity as a non-securities settlement layer provides durable resilience; only Bitcoin's unmatched hash rate and store-of-value narrative rank higher.

forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.

These rates are based purely on quality and network strength — treat them as a rough guide, not a precise prediction.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Fee-based methods (Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue) used DeFiLlama protocol fee data to anchor the valuation. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Metcalfe Network Value is shown in the breakdown chart without contributing to the median: the implied IV is below 10% of current price, indicating the token is trading at a significant premium to its network activity level. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment did not fire: FDV/MC is within the acceptable range for this quality tier, so no dilution penalty was applied. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.

metric_assessments.fee_growth: Ethereum's perfect 10 reflects its position as the largest fee-generating protocol in crypto, consistently capturing billions in annual transaction fees ($2.4B annually). The protocol benefits from network effects—more users and applications on Ethereum drive more demand for blockspace, which naturally increases fee capture. However, competition from Layer 2 solutions and alternative chains is fragmenting transaction volume away from the base layer, preventing an even higher demonstrated growth trajectory.
metric_assessments.capital_efficiency: With a 104.62 MC-to-fees ratio, Ethereum generates roughly 1% of its market cap in annual fees—a ratio that's tight by traditional software standards but reasonable given crypto's growth phase. This efficiency stems from Ethereum's dominance: it commands ~$250B in market value while extracting substantial fees from DeFi, NFTs, and general usage without relying on artificial scarcity tactics. The score stops at 9 because the ratio, while healthy, isn't exceptional enough to suggest Ethereum is extracting maximum value from its user base relative to its valuation.
metric_assessments.holder_value_accrual: Only 35.3% of Ethereum's fees flow directly to ETH holders through staking rewards and burns, yielding an 8 rather than a higher score. Post-merge, the protocol does burn transaction fees and distribute staking yields, which is meaningful passive income relative to few other crypto assets. The score reflects that roughly two-thirds of fee value either goes to validators as operational rewards or doesn't accrue to token holders at all, limiting the economic moat that fee capture would otherwise create for ETH owners.
metric_assessments.network_demand: The 5.03% volume-to-market-cap ratio signals that Ethereum processes meaningful daily transaction volume relative to its size, but the score of 6 acknowledges this is moderate, not exceptional. Strong fundamental demand exists—Ethereum remains the settlement layer for DeFi, staking, and institutional activity—but this demand isn't accelerating rapidly enough to significantly increase fees per unit of capital deployed. Layer 2 scaling and the existence of cheaper alternatives means marginal new demand isn't concentrating on Ethereum's base layer as it once did.
metric_assessments.dilution_control: Ethereum scores a perfect 10 because 100% of its circulating supply is already in circulation with no additional token issuance scheduled—there is zero future dilution risk to existing holders. The circulating and fully diluted valuations are identical (FDV/MC = 1.0), meaning no hidden supply surprises exist. This stands in stark contrast to many crypto projects with large unallocated token pools, making Ethereum's supply certainty a genuine structural advantage for long-term holders.
metric_assessments.protocol_maturity: Ranked #2 globally by market cap after Bitcoin, Ethereum's position reflects a decade of proven operation, established developer ecosystems, and immense institutional adoption—the perfect score captures this standing. The protocol has survived multiple market cycles, major upgrades (the merge), and sustained competition while maintaining its position as crypto's primary smart contract platform. No meaningful maturity concerns exist; the only reason this isn't cited as a weakness is that maturity itself isn't a limiting factor here.

quality_narrative.classification_reason: Ethereum's Safe rating rests on three pillars: a quality score of 8.9 driven by perfect marks in dilution control and protocol maturity, capital efficiency ratios that confirm sustainable fee generation ($2.4B annually), and proven network resilience over a decade. The classification acknowledges that while fee distribution to holders (35.3%) and network demand growth (5.03% volume/MC) leave room for improvement, Ethereum's fundamental position as the #2 protocol with zero supply dilution and massive institutional anchoring makes it fundamentally lower-risk than speculative alternatives.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: Ethereum is a cash-flow generating protocol ranked #2 globally that captures billions in annual transaction fees from its dominant position in DeFi, staking, and institutional settlement. Its core strength is network dominance—so much economic activity depends on Ethereum that fee capture is almost inevitable, and the perfect dilution control (100% circulating, no future issuance) ensures those fees accrue to a fixed token supply. The main risk is that Layer 2 solutions and competing chains are fragmenting blockspace demand away from the base layer, meaning fee growth may plateau even as Ethereum remains essential infrastructure. Token supply is fully distributed with zero dilution ahead, eliminating a major risk vector plaguing other projects. For risk-conscious investors seeking exposure to crypto's most established cash-generating asset, Ethereum offers proven economics and deep liquidity, though at current valuations (104x fee ratio), much of the upside likely depends on sustained fee growth acceleration rather than multiple expansion.
quality_narrative.quality_conclusion: Ethereum scores 8.9/10 (Safe) as a Cash-Flow Protocol. The overall quality profile reflects the protocol's maturity, fee economics, and token supply dynamics relative to peers.

coin_profile.name: Ethereum Classic
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Store of Value asset is a cryptocurrency whose main purpose is to hold and protect your wealth over time — the same way people have used gold for thousands of years. It does not earn fees from users. Instead, its value comes from scarcity, trust, and the belief that it will be worth more in the future. Bitcoin is the most well-known example. We value these assets by comparing them to gold and looking at the cost of producing new coins.
coin_profile.description: Ethereum Classic is the original Ethereum chain that continued after the 2016 DAO hack fork. It maintains the original 'code is law' philosophy and is proof-of-work. ETC has a fixed supply schedule and positions itself as a store of value for the Ethereum ecosystem.

metric_assessments.scarcity: ETC scores a 2 out of 10 because 25.7% supply overhang—tokens not yet in circulation—hangs over future price discovery. The protocol has defined issuance mechanics, but the gap between circulating and total supply is substantial enough to meaningfully dilute existing holders if released. This structural headwind makes scarcity claims difficult to defend.
metric_assessments.adoption_momentum: The 0.0066 rank proxy reflects ETC's position outside the top tier of active networks, translating to a score of 3. While the protocol operates and maintains mining activity, there is no measurable momentum in user adoption or ecosystem growth relative to larger peers. Without accelerating adoption signals, momentum remains stalled.
metric_assessments.security_premium: ETC scores a strong 5 on security premium because it maintains proof-of-work consensus with genuine hashrate backing the network. The mining ecosystem provides real production costs and computational security, supporting a legitimate premium over costless digital assets. However, the premium cannot be quantified precisely relative to rivals, capping the score from reaching maximum confidence.
metric_assessments.monetary_premium_quality: A score of 2 reflects the 1.34 FDV-to-market-cap ratio, meaning dilution from unreleased supply is modest but present. The protocol's monetary properties are undermined by this gap—there is latent supply overhang that could suppress long-term price appreciation relative to current market cap. This compression limits how much monetary scarcity the asset can credibly claim.
metric_assessments.dilution_control: ETC scores a 6 because 74.3% of the eventual supply is already circulating, indicating that most dilution is behind the protocol rather than ahead. The remaining 25.7% tail still introduces uncertainty, but the bulk of the token base is locked in. This is a relative strength compared to early-stage projects, though not as tight as truly capped supplies.
metric_assessments.market_resilience: The 1.6% blended 30-day/7-day price volatility score of 4 suggests moderate swings—not extreme panic nor explosive conviction. Over the past month, ETC moved 3.6%, indicating steady but unspectacular price action without either strong accumulation or capitulation signals. Resilience is muted because the asset lacks the conviction or stability seen in tier-one stores of value.

quality_narrative.classification_reason: Ethereum Classic is rated Dangerous because its overall quality score of 3.6/10 (35.7th percentile) reflects multiple compounding weaknesses: adoption momentum is essentially flat, scarcity claims are undermined by 25.7% supply overhang, and monetary premium quality is thin at a 1.34 FDV/MC ratio. A ranked #59 asset in the Store of Value category with below-average scores across adoption and scarcity fundamentals signals elevated risk relative to its intended use case.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: Ethereum Classic is a proof-of-work blockchain forked from Ethereum in 2016, maintaining immutability through mining consensus and offering genuine computational security as a store of value. Its main strength lies in the 74.3% circulating supply already in the wild—most dilution is historical rather than future—and a functional, decentralized mining ecosystem backing the network. The critical risk is adoption stagnation: ranked #59 with negligible adoption momentum, ETC competes in a crowded space without clear differentiation or user growth, making it difficult to justify a premium valuation. Token supply dynamics are mixed; while the FDV/MC ratio of 1.34 is not alarming, the 25.7% supply overhang still introduces dilution uncertainty that could suppress price appreciation. For beginners, ETC is a speculative bet on store-of-value positioning rather than a defensive holding, requiring conviction that proof-of-work heritage and network resilience justify holding it over larger, more adopted alternatives.
quality_narrative.quality_conclusion: Ethereum Classic scores 3.6/10 (Dangerous) as a Store of Value. The overall quality profile reflects the protocol's maturity, fee economics, and token supply dynamics relative to peers.

The protocol may have theoretical mechanisms for returning value—such as buybacks, staking rewards, or fee distribution—but the lack of hard data suggests these mechanisms are either nascent, underutilized, or not transparently tracked. This vagueness is why the score remains at the floor despite the protocol's fee-based positioning.
metric_assessments.network_demand: The score of 1 is justified by extremely weak on-chain traction: only 0.58% volume-to-market-cap ratio, meaning the protocol generates minimal trading or usage activity relative to its valuation. This indicates that despite existing at rank #476, Escoin has failed to build meaningful user engagement or utility adoption—the lifeblood of a cash-flow protocol. The protocol is essentially a shell with a valuation but little economic substance driving that valuation.

However, the still-sizeable unreleased supply (~26%) means some ongoing dilution pressure remains a consideration.
metric_assessments.protocol_maturity: The score of 1 is directly tied to rank #476, placing Escoin far outside the established top tier of proven protocols and indicating it lacks the battle-tested infrastructure, adoption, and credibility of mature systems. forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.

Bear (-21.1%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 53.3% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment did not fire: FDV/MC is within the acceptable range for this quality tier, so no dilution penalty was applied. Only 2 methods contributed to the median. More data sources (DeFiLlama fee history) would improve confidence in this estimate. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.
